title: CRM for B2B SaaS Sales Teams

prd.oneLiner: A CRM that connects lead intake, accounts, opportunities, activities, tasks, and forecasting for B2B SaaS sales teams.
prd.goal: Within six months of launch, increase weekly follow-up coverage per seller by 30% and reduce stale pipeline records to less than 20%.
prd.whyNow: Small and mid-sized SaaS sales teams often outgrow spreadsheets before they can justify an enterprise CRM rollout.
prd.problem: Lead sources, account context, contact relationships, activity history, and next steps are scattered across spreadsheets, email, and project tools. Sellers lose context and miss follow-ups.
prd.solution: Use one account- and opportunity-centered data model to connect lead intake, pipeline management, accounts, contacts, activities, tasks, and sales reporting.
prd.alternatives: Teams combine spreadsheets, email labels, generic project-management tools, or a heavyweight enterprise CRM.
prd.differentiator: The default workflow is designed around the B2B SaaS journey from lead to account, opportunity, activity, next action, and forecast without requiring complex customization.

targets.name: Account Executive
targets.needs: Understand lead and account context quickly and never miss the next action.
targets.pain: Email, call notes, and tasks are fragmented, so follow-up timing is easy to miss.

targets.name: Sales Development Representative
targets.needs: Qualify new leads quickly and hand them to the right owner and pipeline stage.
targets.pain: Lead quality and duplicates are difficult to assess across different sources.

targets.name: Sales Manager
targets.needs: See pipeline health, activity, forecast, and bottlenecks across the team.
targets.pain: Stale opportunities and at-risk deals are discovered too late.

targets.name: Revenue Operations
targets.needs: Keep sales data quality, definitions, permissions, and process rules consistent.
targets.pain: Duplicate records and inconsistent stages make reporting unreliable.

scenarios.text: An SDR reviews leads from web forms and CSV imports, merges duplicates, and assigns qualified leads to the appropriate AE.
An AE adds a new opportunity to the pipeline, reviews account context and past activity, then schedules the next meeting task.
A sales manager reviews conversion, overdue follow-ups, and at-risk deals before coaching the team.
A RevOps administrator updates roles, required fields, and pipeline stages while preserving an audit trail.

prd.northStar: Share of active opportunities with a recorded next action

kpis.name: Follow-up coverage
kpis.method: Weekly share of active opportunities with a due task or scheduled activity

kpis.name: Pipeline freshness
kpis.target: Fewer than 20% of opportunities stale for seven days
kpis.method: Measure the last update to stage, amount, close date, or activity

kpis.name: Lead response time
kpis.target: Qualify 80% of new leads within 24 hours
kpis.method: Time from lead creation to qualification or owner assignment

kpis.name: Forecast accuracy
kpis.method: Compare committed amount with closed-won revenue

inScope: Lead capture, duplicate detection, qualification, and assignment
Pipeline stages, opportunity amounts, probabilities, and close dates
Account and contact profiles with ownership and relationships
Call, email, meeting, and note activity logging
Tasks, due dates, reminders, and seller work queues
Pipeline and activity dashboards, automation, global search, and forecast analysis
Role-based access, team management, and configurable sales process settings
nonGoals: A full marketing automation campaign builder
A replacement for a customer-support ticketing system
A complex CPQ pricing rules engine
assumptions: Initial customers are B2B SaaS sales organizations with 10 to 100 sellers.
Early lead and activity data arrives primarily through manual entry and CSV import.
Pipeline stages and required fields are managed at workspace level.
risks: Data quality and forecast accuracy decline if sellers do not record activities.
Migration from an existing CRM can be a substantial onboarding burden.
Too many mandatory fields can reduce adoption.
Broad email and calendar permissions can delay security review.
openQuestions: How much email and calendar synchronization should the first release support?
Should lead scoring begin as rules-based scoring or manual grading?
How much team, role, and regional permission configuration is required initially?
Which forecast categories and probability rules should be customizable by team?
constraints: The MVP is web-first; mobile web focuses on viewing and simple updates.
Personal and sales-sensitive data must use role-based access.
Core objects require auditable create and update history.

requirements.title: Lead management
requirements.desc: Capture, qualify, de-duplicate, and assign incoming leads.

acceptance.text: A seller can identify lead source, owner, qualification state, and duplicate candidates.

features.title: Lead inbox
features.desc: Collect leads from manual entry, CSV imports, web forms, and APIs in a reviewable inbox.

acceptance.text: Each lead shows source, created date, owner, and processing status.

specs.title: Create and import leads
specs.desc: Create a lead manually or import a batch with source metadata.

acceptance.text: Imported rows with errors are reported before final submission.

features.title: Lead qualification
features.desc: Validate, merge, score, and assign leads before converting them into sales work.

acceptance.text: A qualified lead can be assigned to an AE with an auditable decision.

specs.title: Duplicate review
specs.desc: Detect likely duplicates using email, company domain, and normalized company name.

acceptance.text: A user can merge a duplicate while retaining source history.

requirements.title: Pipeline and opportunities
requirements.desc: Manage a shared sales pipeline and the work around every opportunity.

acceptance.text: A seller can update an opportunity from the pipeline or its detail page.

features.title: Pipeline board
features.desc: Visualize opportunities by stage, amount, close date, probability, and owner.

acceptance.text: The board can filter by team, owner, period, and stage.

specs.title: Stage movement
specs.desc: Move an opportunity between configured sales stages.

acceptance.text: Stage changes preserve previous stage and timestamp in history.

features.title: Opportunity workspace
features.desc: Maintain deal details, stakeholders, timeline, amount, probability, and next action in one view.

acceptance.text: An opportunity shows its account, contacts, related activities, and open tasks.

specs.title: Deal health
specs.desc: Highlight missing next actions, stale activity, and close-date risk.

acceptance.text: A warning links directly to the corrective action.

requirements.title: Account and contact data
requirements.desc: Maintain trustworthy customer-company and stakeholder context.

acceptance.text: Users can navigate from an account to its contacts and active opportunities.

features.title: Account profiles
features.desc: Store company information, ownership, relationships, and linked opportunities.

acceptance.text: An account profile shows related contacts, open opportunities, and recent activity.

specs.title: Account timeline
specs.desc: Surface account-level activity in chronological order.

acceptance.text: Timeline items link to the originating activity or opportunity.

features.title: Contact management
features.desc: Maintain contact roles, email addresses, relationship context, and engagement history.

acceptance.text: A contact can be linked to an account and multiple opportunities.

specs.title: Contact relationships
specs.desc: Show the contact role and influence for each linked opportunity.

acceptance.text: Users can add and update opportunity-specific contact roles.

requirements.title: Activities and follow-up tasks
requirements.desc: Make customer engagement and next actions visible and actionable.

acceptance.text: A seller can create a follow-up task from an activity, account, contact, or opportunity.

features.title: Activity timeline
features.desc: Record calls, emails, meetings, and notes against the relevant customer context.

acceptance.text: Activities are visible on linked account, contact, and opportunity timelines.

specs.title: Activity logging
specs.desc: Capture type, time, participants, summary, and related objects.

acceptance.text: A user can log an activity without leaving the current object.

features.title: Task management
features.desc: Create, complete, defer, and reassign personal and team follow-up work.

acceptance.text: Users can distinguish their own, team, and overdue tasks.

specs.title: Task reminders
specs.desc: Set due dates, reminders, and optional recurrence.

acceptance.text: Incomplete tasks past their due date appear as overdue.

requirements.title: Insights and operating controls
requirements.desc: Give sales leaders and RevOps the information and controls needed to run the sales process.

acceptance.text: A manager can move from a summary metric to the underlying sales records.

features.title: Sales dashboard
features.desc: Summarize pipeline value, conversion, lead handling, activity, and overdue follow-up.

acceptance.text: Metrics update for the selected period, team, owner, and product filters.

specs.title: Pipeline summary
specs.desc: Show opportunity count, amount, weighted value, and expected close period by stage.

acceptance.text: A summary card opens the corresponding filtered pipeline view.

features.title: Operational alerts
features.desc: Collect at-risk opportunities, unprocessed leads, overdue tasks, and data gaps in one queue.

acceptance.text: Every alert includes its reason, owner, affected object, and recommended action.

specs.title: Risk rules
specs.desc: Detect long inactivity, missing next action, imminent close dates, and required-field gaps.

acceptance.text: Each alert shows the triggering rule and last checked time.

features.title: Sales process settings
features.desc: Configure pipeline stages, required fields, lead sources, task types, and notification rules.

acceptance.text: An administrator sees the expected impact of a setting change before saving it.

specs.title: Pipeline configuration
specs.desc: Define stage names, order, probability, and movement requirements.

acceptance.text: Removing an active stage requires selecting a destination stage.

features.title: Role-based access
features.desc: Control access to accounts, opportunities, activities, and dashboards by role, team, and ownership.

acceptance.text: Restricted users see only permitted data in lists, details, and search.

specs.title: Role templates
specs.desc: Provide starter roles for administrator, sales manager, AE, SDR, and RevOps.

acceptance.text: Each role differentiates view, create, edit, delete, and export access.

features.title: Sales automation and global search
features.desc: Automate repetitive follow-up and find relevant CRM objects from one search surface.

acceptance.text: A user can move directly from a result or automation event to its related record.

specs.title: Automation rules
specs.desc: Create conditions for lead intake, stage changes, inactivity, approaching due dates, and field changes.

acceptance.text: Rules show expected volume before activation.

specs.title: Global search
specs.desc: Search accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, activities, and tasks with access-aware results.

acceptance.text: Top results are keyboard-accessible and show record type and last update.

features.title: Sales forecast
features.desc: Manage commit categories, expected wins, pipeline coverage, and at-risk deals.

acceptance.text: Forecast totals can be filtered by period, team, owner, and product line.

specs.title: Forecast drill-down
specs.desc: Open the opportunities and risk reasons behind a forecast total.

acceptance.text: Drill-down preserves the active forecast filters and commit category.
